long_description: Fuel system leak develops in flight, causing abnormal fuel quantity decrease, possible imbalance, and elevated fire risk.
pilot_actions: Monitor fuel state and imbalance, shut off affected feed path if possible, avoid afterburner, declare emergency, and divert to nearest suitable airfield.

long_description: In-flight fuel feed pressure drops below engine demand, increasing risk of surge, stall, or flameout during high-power operation.
pilot_actions: Reduce power demand, avoid negative-g or aggressive maneuvering, monitor engine response, and recover using the shortest safe route.

long_description: A fuel transfer valve fails in flight, trapping usable fuel in one tank group and creating imbalance or premature feed limitations.
pilot_actions: Monitor imbalance, select alternate transfer mode if available, limit maneuvering that worsens feed issues, and divert before trapped fuel becomes operationally critical.

long_description: A fuel quantity sensing channel fails in flight, causing misleading fuel state, imbalance indications, or unreliable endurance planning.
pilot_actions: Cross-check totalizer trends with engine time and tank behavior, use conservative fuel planning, and recover before reserve uncertainty becomes unacceptable.

long_description: Crossfeed control fails in flight, preventing normal balancing or alternate feed routing after a tank or line abnormality.
pilot_actions: Avoid maneuvers that worsen imbalance, conserve fuel, and divert before the loss of feed flexibility threatens engine supply.

long_description: A boost pump fails in flight, reducing tank feed margin and increasing susceptibility to pressure loss during maneuvering or low-fuel conditions.
pilot_actions: Reduce maneuver severity, avoid negative-g, monitor engine behavior closely, and divert before feed margin is exhausted.

long_description: Fuel contamination reaches the engine feed path in flight, increasing the risk of rough running, filter restriction, or combustion instability.
pilot_actions: Reduce power demand, monitor for engine anomalies, and terminate the sortie before contamination causes feed or combustion failure.

long_description: A tank imbalance develops in flight outside normal control limits, affecting aircraft handling and reducing usable fuel flexibility.
pilot_actions: Limit maneuvers that worsen imbalance, apply balancing procedures if available, and divert before controllability or feed risk increases.

long_description: A fuel feed shutoff or metering-related valve becomes stuck in flight, restricting normal routing and threatening sustained engine supply.
pilot_actions: Hold a stable power setting, avoid configuration changes that increase feed demand, and recover before the restricted path causes flameout risk.

long_description: Fuel tank venting degrades in flight, causing abnormal tank pressure behavior, transfer problems, or misleading quantity trends.
pilot_actions: Avoid prolonged high-demand operation, monitor quantity behavior conservatively, and land before vent-related feed issues worsen.
